mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points within a single month signals an imminent recession, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates compresses real household purchasing power and equity risk premiums, jointly driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-factor model in out-of-sample Sharpe ratios.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during a post-shock recovery phase expands corporate capacity utilization and earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant correlation with INDPRO momentum, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained environments.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 1400 thousand units signals a broad demand shock and credit tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as financial conditions deteriorate.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when HOUST drops below the 1400 threshold, or the signal exhibits a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in retail sales indicates sustained consumer spending power, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day investment horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between RSAFS momentum and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth occurring alongside moderating consumer price inflation confirms a soft-landing scenario, which expands equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS exceeds 200k and CPIAUCSL decelerates, or the interaction effect disappears in a multivariate factor model controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: In a peak tightening regime, further federal funds rate hikes despite persistently high consumer price inflation trigger aggressive valuation compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat following simultaneous rate hikes and sticky inflation prints, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when real rates turn negative.
